title: Massachusetts Law About Sick Leave — Trial Court Law Libraries
description: MA Trial Court Law Libraries research guide surveying the legal landscape of sick leave in Massachusetts: the earned sick time statute (G.L. c.149 §§148C–148D) and its regulations (940 CMR 33), selected SJC and federal cases interpreting the law (including federal preemption for airline/railroad employees), and curated secondary sources. An annotated legal bibliography rather than a plain-language how-to.
notes: Publisher is the Trial Court Law Libraries (NOT the AGO). Notable cases: Bodge v. Commonwealth, 494 Mass. 623 (2024) — c.175M does not require employers to guarantee sick/vacation accrual during PFML leave; Mui v. Massport, 478 Mass. 710 (2018) — accrued unused sick time is not 'wages' under the Wage Act. Federal preemption for airline (ADA) and railroad (RUIA) employees. Page last updated Mar 4 2026 (in-file).
